description: Baseline: compare Colorful Times startup/setup against under-50-LOC minimal switcher across three scenarios

asi.hypothesis: Establish fair baseline for startup/setup delta versus a minimal schedule-aware colorscheme switcher; no production optimization yet.
asi.benchmark_design: Primary uses cleared-module require plus setup for empty, two-entry, and disabled scenarios; persist=false and isolated XDG avoid user-state effects.
asi.next_action_hint: Inspect setup path costs: lazy core require, validation, deepcopy/base config copy, autocmd registration, and command registration.

description: Lazy-load state and system modules from core to avoid unnecessary setup-time requires

asi.hypothesis: Core setup with persist=false and non-system backgrounds does not need state.lua or system.lua at require time; lazy getters should cut startup delta without changing behavior.
asi.result: Primary delta dropped from 460.9us to 41.1us; Colorful Times setup is about 1.09x the minimal switcher in this benchmark.
asi.tradeoff: Runtime calls that actually need persistence or system detection now pay one cached function call plus first require at point of use; tests passed.
asi.next_action_hint: Remaining delta likely comes from schedule.lua eager require, validation, base config deepcopy, and autocmd/defer setup. Explore simple no-op reductions only.

description: Lazy-load schedule module and only require it when validation or runtime schedule resolution needs it

asi.hypothesis: Schedule.lua is not needed for empty-schedule setup and can be required on first validation/resolution, cutting average setup cost across mixed scenarios.
asi.result: Primary delta improved from 41.1us to -12.0us; benchmark now shows Colorful Times setup at about 0.97x minimal reference speed, within likely noise.
asi.tradeoff: Hot resolution path adds one cached schedule() getter call; secondary resolve/apply metrics remain comparable and checks passed.
asi.next_action_hint: Rerun current state for noise confidence before deeper changes; remaining differences likely below benchmark noise floor.

description: Cache lazy schedule module in validation loop instead of calling getter for every schedule entry

asi.hypothesis: After lazy-loading schedule.lua, validation of non-empty schedules should cache the module in a local so each entry does not pay a getter call.
asi.result: Metric improved from -12.0us to -22.1us, though likely near noise floor; code simplification is still low risk.
asi.tradeoff: No behavior change; local cache only affects validation loop. Checks passed.
asi.next_action_hint: Further startup gains may be noise. Consider improving benchmark confidence or targeting command registration only if it remains measurable.

description: Store parsed start/stop locals inside schedule.validate_entry to avoid repeated parse_time calls

asi.hypothesis: Avoiding repeated parse_time calls in validate_entry should reduce setup cost for non-empty schedules.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary delta worsened versus current best (-22.08us to -3.22us), likely because extra branching/locals outweighed cached parse_time savings under LuaJIT/noisy microbenchmarks.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not retry this exact validate_entry rewrite; repeated parse_time calls are already cheap due _time_cache.

description: Replace vim.tbl_deep_extend default merge with manual field assignment

asi.hypothesis: Manual default merge might avoid vim.tbl_deep_extend overhead in setup.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary delta regressed from -22.08us to +25.92us and the change narrowed supported merge behavior for unknown default keys.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep vim.tbl_deep_extend for default config; C-side/helper overhead is not the bottleneck here.

description: Create ColorfulTimes augroup lazily in setup to avoid module-load augroup work

asi.hypothesis: Moving augroup creation from core module load into setup and avoiding the first extra clear_autocmds call might reduce setup cost.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary delta regressed badly from -22.08us to +228.88us; moving augroup work into setup appears slower under the measured path or disrupts autocmd caching.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not lazily create the augroup. Keep existing top-level create_augroup plus setup clear_autocmds pattern.

description: Cache vim.api in core for augroup and autocmd setup calls

asi.hypothesis: Localizing vim.api might reduce global/table lookup overhead during setup autocmd registration.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary delta worsened versus best (-22.08us to +6.93us); global lookup overhead is not material, and added local did not help.
asi.next_action_hint: Avoid micro-localizing vim.api for this path; benchmark noise plus extra local can offset any lookup savings.

description: Validate setup schedules with a lightweight core-local validator to avoid loading schedule.lua at startup

asi.hypothesis: Setup only needs validation, not schedule preprocessing; a small core-local validator can preserve synchronous config errors while deferring schedule.lua load to deferred runtime work.
asi.result: Primary delta improved from -22.08us to -75.26us; Colorful Times setup measured about 0.83x the minimal switcher.
asi.tradeoff: Validation rules are now mirrored in core and schedule.lua, so future schedule validation changes must keep both paths aligned. Tests passed.
asi.next_action_hint: Add targeted regression tests if validation rules change; next explore whether benchmark/docs need note about duplicated setup validator.

description: Localize nvim_create_user_command in plugin command registration

asi.hypothesis: Caching vim.api.nvim_create_user_command as a local should reduce plugin command registration cost.
asi.rollback_reason: Although command_us improved versus recent runs, primary delta worsened from -75.26us to -60.69us, and primary metric remains the optimization target.
asi.next_action_hint: Command registration is not worth changing unless primary includes plugin/ startup; keep explicit command registration for now.

name: Match minimal colorscheme switcher apply speed

description: Baseline for apply/switch parity after setup startup reached minimal-switcher speed

asi.hypothesis: Reset target to actual apply/switch cost now that startup/setup delta is below the minimal switcher baseline.
asi.next_action_hint: Inspect apply_colorscheme and resolve_theme_context for work not present in minimal apply, especially scheduling, default override resolution, and first schedule preprocess.

description: Resolve hot apply path into scalar values to avoid allocating context tables on every switch

asi.hypothesis: apply_colorscheme only needs scalar resolution fields; building a context table each call adds overhead not present in the minimal switcher.
asi.result: apply_delta_us improved from 17.76us to 4.37us while startup guardrail stayed faster than minimal (delta_us=-94.49us).
asi.tradeoff: resolve_theme_context still returns the public table by wrapping scalar resolution; pinned path still deepcopies session_pin only for context callers.
asi.next_action_hint: Remaining apply delta is small. Inspect schedule preprocessing on first apply and closure allocation from vim.schedule.

description: Apply colorschemes synchronously outside fast events while preserving scheduled fast-event safety

asi.hypothesis: vim.schedule closure allocation adds overhead for normal main-thread apply calls; only fast-event/async contexts require scheduling.
asi.result: apply_delta_us improved from 4.37us to -11.35us; startup guardrail also stayed faster than minimal.
asi.tradeoff: Public apply now performs immediate Vim API work when not in a fast event. Fast-event callers still go through vim.schedule, satisfying async safety rules.
asi.next_action_hint: Verify no user-facing docs promise asynchronous apply semantics. Future apply optimizations should avoid weakening fast-event safety.

description: Cache vim.in_fast_event in a local for apply_when_safe

asi.hypothesis: Localizing vim.in_fast_event should reduce repeated global lookup cost in apply_when_safe.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary apply_delta_us regressed from -11.35us to +21.92us; lookup localization was not beneficial in this noisy hot path.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep direct vim.in_fast_event access; avoid micro-localizing Vim globals without stronger evidence.

description: Assume Neovim vim.in_fast_event exists and remove nil guard in apply_when_safe

asi.hypothesis: Neovim >=0.12 always has vim.in_fast_event, so removing the existence guard might shrink apply_when_safe overhead.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary apply_delta_us worsened from -11.35us to +7.68us; the extra guard is not the bottleneck and may be optimized well already.
asi.next_action_hint: Leave the compatibility guard in place.

description: Inline apply_when_safe logic in the common non-system apply branch

asi.hypothesis: Inlining the helper in the common non-system branch should avoid one Lua function call per apply.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary apply_delta_us regressed from -11.35us to +13.91us; duplicated branch code was slower under the benchmark.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep the apply_when_safe helper; LuaJIT likely handles the local function better than duplicated conditional code.

description: Cache cfg.default in local variables inside scalar theme resolution

asi.hypothesis: Localizing cfg.default should reduce repeated table indexing in default and disabled resolution paths.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary apply_delta_us regressed from -11.35us to +14.71us and startup guardrail weakened; extra locals did not help.
asi.next_action_hint: Avoid table-index micro-optimizations in resolve_theme_parts unless profiling shows a clear hotspot.

description: Skip schedule module lookup when enabled config has an empty schedule

asi.hypothesis: Empty schedules can resolve directly to defaults without loading or consulting schedule.lua, reducing apply/resolve work for common configs.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary apply_delta_us improved versus segment baseline but worsened versus current best (-11.35us to +1.83us); under the apply target, this is not worth keeping now.
asi.next_action_hint: The empty-schedule fast path may be worth revisiting if benchmark separates cold first-apply from steady-state apply, but do not retry unchanged.

description: No-code confirmation rerun of current apply-speed best state

asi.hypothesis: Measure noise in the current best apply-speed state before trying more micro-optimizations.
asi.rollback_reason: No production change was made and apply_delta_us reran much worse than the current best; the apply benchmark is noisy near parity.
asi.next_action_hint: Treat small apply_delta_us wins cautiously. Prefer structural improvements or repeated confirmation before keeping micro-optimizations.

description: Revert apply_when_safe to always schedule as an A/B check against synchronous safe apply

asi.hypothesis: A/B check whether synchronous safe apply was a noisy win by reverting to unconditional vim.schedule.
asi.rollback_reason: apply_delta_us=11.77us is worse than the synchronous safe-apply best (-11.35us), so keep the current apply_when_safe behavior despite benchmark noise.
asi.next_action_hint: If apply noise remains high, increase apply iterations or split cold/steady-state metrics before making more micro changes.

name: Match minimal colorscheme switcher stable apply speed

description: Baseline for stable apply benchmark with higher apply iteration count

asi.hypothesis: Increase apply iterations from 20 to 100 and reduce samples from 31 to 21 to lower apply_delta_us noise while keeping iteration time under 10s.
asi.benchmark_change: Workload changed, so experiment was reinitialized. Metrics are not directly comparable to the previous apply segment.
asi.next_action_hint: Re-test structural apply changes under the stabler apply benchmark; avoid keeping micro wins without confirmation.

description: Use unconditional vim.schedule again for apply after stable benchmark showed lower apply delta

asi.hypothesis: The prior synchronous-safe apply win may have been noise; under the stabler apply benchmark, unconditional scheduling may be as fast or faster while preserving old async semantics.
asi.result: apply_delta_us improved from stable baseline +18.80us to -16.01us; startup guardrail remains faster than minimal.
asi.tradeoff: This reverts the public timing semantics back to always-scheduled apply, reducing behavior-change risk from the prior experiment.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep stable benchmark settings. Re-test scalar resolution separately if needed; focus on structural apply work, not scheduling micro-changes.

description: Inline unconditional vim.schedule calls and remove apply helper

asi.hypothesis: After reverting to unconditional scheduling, removing the helper might avoid one Lua call and simplify apply.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary apply_delta_us regressed from -16.01us to +15.91us; helper form benchmarks better under stable settings.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep the small apply helper even though it only wraps vim.schedule.

description: No-code confirmation rerun of stable apply best state

asi.hypothesis: Confirm stable apply best after reverting to unconditional scheduling.
asi.rollback_reason: No production change was made and apply_delta_us reran worse than the current best (-16.01us), though still better than stable baseline.
asi.next_action_hint: Stable apply benchmark still has noise around parity; treat sub-10us changes cautiously.

name: Match minimal colorscheme switcher paired apply speed

description: Baseline paired apply benchmark using per-sample Colorful Times minus minimal deltas

asi.hypothesis: Paired apply deltas should reduce noise from subtracting independent medians and better show whether Colorful Times matches the minimal switcher.
asi.benchmark_change: Workload changed again; apply_delta_us now uses the median of paired per-sample deltas. Previous stable segment best is not directly comparable.
asi.next_action_hint: With paired apply delta essentially at parity, focus only on structural changes or stop apply micro-tuning unless a clear bottleneck appears.

description: Retry empty-schedule fast path under paired apply benchmark

asi.hypothesis: Paired apply benchmark might show a real win from resolving empty schedules directly to defaults without loading schedule.lua.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary apply_delta_us worsened from -0.055us to +3.585us, so the fast path still does not help the active metric.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not retry the empty-schedule fast path for apply unless the benchmark is split into explicit empty-schedule and non-empty-schedule workloads.

description: A/B check table-based resolve context in apply and resolve_theme under paired benchmark

asi.hypothesis: Confirm scalar resolution is still useful under the paired apply benchmark by reverting hot apply/resolve calls to table-based context resolution.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary apply_delta_us worsened from -0.055us to +8.64us; scalar resolution remains beneficial.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep resolve_theme_parts for hot paths.

name: Match minimal switcher with hourly schedule coverage

description: Baseline after adding representative 24-entry hourly schedule scenario

asi.hypothesis: Broaden workload with a representative 24-entry hourly schedule to reduce overfitting to tiny schedules while keeping the minimal reference unchanged.
asi.benchmark_change: Workload changed; experiment reinitialized. Primary is paired apply_delta_us across four scenarios: empty schedule, two-entry day/night, disabled one-entry, and 24-entry hourly schedule.
asi.next_action_hint: Large-schedule scenario may expose schedule lookup/preprocess costs. Explore schedule runtime logic only if it improves paired apply without hurting startup parity.

description: Inline active-entry range check and use numeric loop in schedule.get_active_entry

asi.hypothesis: Large hourly schedules pay one helper call per entry during active lookup; inlining range checks and using a numeric loop should reduce apply/resolve cost.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary apply_delta_us worsened from 3.08us to 9.74us; LuaJIT/Neovim path did not benefit from the inline numeric-loop rewrite.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep schedule.get_active_entry helper structure. Do not retry this exact inline loop optimization.

description: Precompute parsed schedule during setup when persistence is disabled

asi.hypothesis: Moving schedule preprocessing from first apply into setup for persist=false configs may reduce apply_delta_us for non-empty and hourly schedules while keeping behavior unchanged.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary apply_delta_us worsened from 3.08us to 10.08us and startup guardrail regressed past the minimal switcher (delta_us=52.41us, ratio=1.11).
asi.next_action_hint: Do not precompute parsed schedules during setup under this target; lazy preprocessing preserves startup parity and does not hurt paired apply enough to justify the tradeoff.

description: Use direct array appends instead of table.insert in schedule.preprocess

asi.hypothesis: schedule.preprocess runs on first apply for non-empty schedules; direct array appends should be cheaper than table.insert while preserving behavior.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary apply_delta_us worsened from 3.08us to 5.35us; direct appends did not help this workload.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep table.insert in schedule.preprocess. Avoid retrying pure append micro-optimization.

description: Cache active schedule entry for non-overlapping schedules

asi.hypothesis: Common schedules are non-overlapping; detecting that in preprocess and caching the active entry should reduce repeated active lookup during apply loops without changing overlapping-schedule semantics.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary apply_delta_us worsened from 3.08us to 8.05us. Non-overlap detection/preprocess overhead and cache checks outweighed lookup savings in this workload.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not add active-entry caching unless a separate real-world profile shows repeated apply calls within one schedule interval dominate.

name: Reduce command registration overhead after switcher parity

description: Baseline command registration cost after setup/apply parity and hourly schedule benchmark broadened

asi.hypothesis: After setup and apply reached parity with the minimal switcher, plugin command registration is the remaining measured startup-adjacent cost worth checking.
asi.benchmark_change: Metric changed to command_us; apply_delta_us and delta_us are guardrails only.
asi.next_action_hint: Explore plugin/colorful-times.lua command registration structure. Avoid removing commands or changing public command behavior.

description: Cache nvim_create_user_command local while registering plugin commands

asi.hypothesis: Command registration calls the same Vim API function six times; localizing it should reduce plugin/ startup overhead without changing public commands.
asi.result: command_us improved from 80.75us to 57.04us; setup/apply guardrails remained within parity targets.
asi.tradeoff: Only plugin/colorful-times.lua changes; no behavior or command list changes.
asi.next_action_hint: Next try reducing closure duplication in command callbacks, but avoid removing commands or requiring core at plugin load.

description: Generate common command callbacks through a helper function

asi.hypothesis: A helper that creates callbacks for common commands might reduce plugin file size and repeated closure bodies during command registration.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary command_us worsened from 57.04us to 61.29us; helper indirection/calls outweighed any reduction in repeated callback bodies.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep explicit callbacks. Further command optimization should target opts table allocation or status callback size, not helper-generated callbacks.

description: Move ColorfulTimesStatus formatting into a lazily required status module

asi.hypothesis: Status command has the largest callback body in plugin/colorful-times.lua; moving formatting to a lazy module should shrink plugin sourcing and command registration cost.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary command_us worsened from 57.04us to 75.58us, likely because the extra require target/closure did not reduce command creation overhead enough and the new file is irrelevant until execution.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep status formatting inline in plugin. Command registration cost is dominated by nvim_create_user_command calls and noise, not callback body size.

description: Localize require in plugin command callbacks

asi.hypothesis: Making require an upvalue for command callbacks might reduce closure/global lookup overhead when registering commands.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary command_us worsened from 57.04us to 63.96us; localizing require only adds plugin-load work and does not help registration.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not localize callback-only globals in plugin/colorful-times.lua.

description: Baseline stable command benchmark averaging ten registration cycles per sample

asi.hypothesis: Average command_us across 10 command registration/delete cycles per sample to reduce noise before further command micro-optimizations.
asi.benchmark_change: Metric implementation changed but still measures plugin/colorful-times.lua command registration cost; experiment reinitialized.
asi.next_action_hint: Retest command registration structure changes under stable command benchmark before keeping or discarding.

description: Retest generated command callbacks under stable command benchmark

asi.hypothesis: The callback helper might perform differently under the stabilized command benchmark averaging ten command registration cycles per sample.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary command_us worsened from 56.83us to 60.35us; explicit callbacks remain faster and simpler.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not retry helper-generated command callbacks.

description: Remove redundant explicit core require from command callbacks

asi.hypothesis: Command callbacks can rely on the public module lazy loader to require core, making explicit require('colorful-times.core') redundant and shrinking callback bodies.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary command_us worsened from 56.83us to 58.82us; smaller callback bodies did not improve registration cost under the stable benchmark.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep explicit core requires in callbacks for clarity and avoid retrying callback-body-only reductions.

description: Reuse one mutable command opts table for user command descriptions

asi.hypothesis: Reusing one opts table while changing desc before each nvim_create_user_command call may reduce table allocations during plugin sourcing while preserving descriptions if Neovim copies opts immediately.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary command_us worsened from 56.83us to 59.44us and the mutable opts pattern is less clear/riskier than literals.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep separate opts table literals for command descriptions.

description: No-code confirmation rerun of stable command best state

asi.hypothesis: Confirm stable command best before switching away from command micro-optimization.
asi.rollback_reason: No production change was made and command_us reran worse than the best 56.83us; current command best remains plausible but near microbenchmark noise.
asi.next_action_hint: Command registration path appears exhausted. Prefer returning to broader setup/apply guardrails or finalizing rather than chasing sub-2us command noise.

name: Revisit startup delta with hourly schedule coverage

description: Baseline startup delta after command path exhausted under broadened benchmark

asi.hypothesis: Return primary target to setup/startup delta after command registration path became noise-limited, keeping apply and command metrics as guardrails.
asi.benchmark_change: Metric changed back to delta_us on the broadened four-scenario workload with stable command measurement still present.
asi.next_action_hint: Try low-risk setup copy improvements such as noref deepcopy; avoid repeating discarded shallow-copy/manual-merge rewrites.

description: Use noref deepcopy for setup config copies

asi.hypothesis: Setup config tables are acyclic; passing noref=true to vim.deepcopy should reduce reference-tracking overhead for user opts and base config copies without changing observable config values.
asi.result: delta_us improved from -84.24us to -90.17us; startup ratio improved to 0.81 while apply and command guardrails remained acceptable.
asi.tradeoff: Shared-table identity inside configs is no longer preserved across deepcopy, which should not matter for Colorful Times config semantics. Tests passed.
asi.next_action_hint: Consider applying noref deepcopy to reload/base-config reset if it affects user-triggered reload, but primary startup path is covered.

description: No-code confirmation rerun of noref deepcopy setup optimization

asi.hypothesis: Confirm whether the noref deepcopy startup improvement is stable or noise.
asi.rollback_reason: No code change was made and delta_us reran much worse (+56.72us vs best -90.17us), indicating startup delta remains noisy in this segment.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not chase small delta_us changes without repeated confirmation or a structural reason. Consider increasing startup samples or using paired startup deltas like apply_delta_us.

name: Paired startup delta with hourly schedule coverage

description: Baseline paired startup delta benchmark after noref deepcopy optimization

asi.hypothesis: Pair Colorful Times and minimal startup samples before taking the median delta to reduce noise from independent startup medians.
asi.benchmark_change: delta_us is now median of paired per-sample startup deltas. Experiment reinitialized; prior delta_us values are not directly comparable.
asi.next_action_hint: Retest noref deepcopy by reverting it under the paired benchmark, or look for structural setup wins larger than startup noise.

description: Revert noref deepcopy after paired startup benchmark showed regular deepcopy faster

asi.hypothesis: The noref deepcopy improvement was likely noise under independent-median startup delta; paired startup benchmark should verify whether regular deepcopy or noref is actually better.
asi.result: Regular vim.deepcopy improved paired delta_us from -76.97us to -107.46us, so noref deepcopy was reverted.
asi.tradeoff: Command guardrail worsened to 63.34us but remains below the original command baseline; apply guardrail remains near parity.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep regular deepcopy in setup. Avoid noref deepcopy for startup unless a stronger repeated signal appears.

description: Cache core-local setup time validation results

asi.hypothesis: The 24-entry scenario repeats time strings across start/stop fields; caching core-local validate_time results should reduce setup validation work without loading schedule.lua.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary paired delta_us worsened from -107.46us to -80.83us; cache table lookups and sentinel handling outweighed repeated parse savings.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep simple uncached core-local validate_time. Do not retry time-validation caching unless schedules are much larger and separately targeted.

description: Register FocusLost and FocusGained with one combined autocmd callback

asi.hypothesis: Combining focus autocmd registration into one nvim_create_autocmd call may reduce setup API overhead while preserving events and behavior.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary paired delta_us worsened from -107.46us to -75.27us and command guardrail worsened; the combined callback/API form is slower here.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep separate FocusLost and FocusGained autocmds.

description: Add explicit setup wrapper in init.lua to avoid metatable __index on setup access

asi.hypothesis: Defining a direct M.setup wrapper might avoid the __index metamethod lookup for the common setup call and reduce startup delta.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary paired delta_us worsened versus best (-107.46us to -98.06us); wrapper call overhead outweighed avoiding __index.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep metatable lazy loading for setup. Do not add explicit wrappers unless targeting a different workload.

description: Unroll default theme validation and remove THEME_KEYS table from core

asi.hypothesis: Validation checks only light and dark theme overrides; unrolling avoids ipairs and a core-local THEME_KEYS allocation during setup.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary paired delta_us worsened from -107.46us to -75.19us; the unrolled code did not help and reduced maintainability.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep THEME_KEYS loop in core validation.

description: No-code confirmation rerun of paired startup best state after discarded micro-optimizations

asi.hypothesis: Confirm current paired startup best after multiple discarded startup micro-optimizations.
asi.rollback_reason: No production change was made and delta_us reran worse than best -107.46us, though still faster than the minimal switcher baseline.
asi.next_action_hint: Startup delta remains noisy around parity. Avoid keeping micro-optimizations unless they repeatedly beat the current code or improve code simplicity.

description: Replace lazy-key lookup table with inline string comparisons

asi.hypothesis: Avoiding the lazy-key table allocation in init.lua might reduce module startup cost while setup key checks short-circuit early.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary paired delta_us worsened versus best (-107.46us to -96.07us), and the inline comparisons are less maintainable than the lookup table.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep the _lazy_keys table.

description: Use package.loaded fallback before requiring public module in core.lua

asi.hypothesis: core.lua is usually loaded from init.lua after the public module is already in package.loaded; checking package.loaded first might avoid require overhead.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary paired delta_us worsened from -107.46us to -76.60us; the package.loaded branch added overhead and was less clear.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep direct require('colorful-times') in core.lua.

description: Skip clearing ColorfulTimes autocmd group on first setup after module-load clear

asi.hypothesis: core.lua already creates the ColorfulTimes augroup with clear=true at module load, so the first setup can skip nvim_clear_autocmds and only clear on later setup calls.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary paired delta_us worsened from current best -107.46us to -75.68us. The extra state branch outweighed skipping the clear call and slightly changed edge-case behavior.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep unconditional setup-time nvim_clear_autocmds.

description: Skip deferred setup callback when persist=false and plugin starts disabled

asi.hypothesis: For persist=false and enabled=false, deferred setup callback only calls load_persisted_state and returns; skipping it should preserve behavior and reduce disabled-scenario setup cost.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary paired delta_us worsened versus best (-107.46us to -94.54us); branch and behavior split did not pay off in the four-scenario benchmark.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep unconditional defer setup path for simpler behavior.

description: Remove explicit nil defaults from public config table

asi.hypothesis: Explicit nil fields in init.lua default config are not observable in Lua tables; removing them and using an empty themes table should reduce table construction/parser work without changing behavior.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary paired delta_us worsened versus best (-107.46us to -94.98us). The source simplification did not improve the active metric.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep explicit nil defaults for readability/documentation.

name: Stable paired startup delta with hourly schedule coverage

description: Baseline stable paired startup benchmark with five startup cycles per sample

asi.hypothesis: Average five startup cycles per sample before paired delta_us calculation to reduce startup measurement noise while keeping the benchmark fair to both implementations.
asi.benchmark_change: Metric implementation changed; delta_us still measures paired startup/setup difference across the same four scenarios, now with CT_BENCH_STARTUP_ITERS=5.
asi.next_action_hint: Retest structural startup ideas under the stabler benchmark; ignore tiny wins under roughly 5-10us.

description: Retest noref deepcopy under stable paired startup benchmark

asi.hypothesis: With startup noise reduced by five startup cycles per sample, noref deepcopy may show a clearer signal for setup config copies.
asi.result: delta_us improved from -76.89us to -83.80us under the stable paired startup benchmark.
asi.tradeoff: Config table reference identity is not preserved inside copied configs; Colorful Times config semantics should not depend on shared subtable identity. Tests passed.
asi.next_action_hint: Run a no-code confirmation before stacking more micro-optimizations; improvement is about 6.9us, near the ignore-tiny-wins threshold.

description: No-code confirmation of noref deepcopy under stable paired startup benchmark

asi.hypothesis: Confirm the noref deepcopy startup improvement before stacking more changes because the first stable run improved by only ~6.9us.
asi.result: Confirmation produced delta_us=-84.80us, close to the prior -83.80us and still better than baseline -76.89us; improvement appears real enough to keep.
asi.tradeoff: No code change this run; keeps existing noref deepcopy commit. Apply guardrail is slower but still near parity; command guardrail remains better than original command baseline.
asi.next_action_hint: Look for similarly structural setup reductions larger than ~5us; avoid pure lookup micro-optimizations already tried.

description: Use noref deepcopy only for user opts while keeping base config deepcopy regular

asi.hypothesis: The setup user opts copy may benefit from noref, while the already-normalized base config copy may be faster or safer with regular deepcopy.
asi.result: delta_us improved from confirmed noref-both best -84.80us to -90.75us. This suggests noref is useful for user opts but not for base config.
asi.tradeoff: Base config copy preserves reference identity again; opts copy still assumes user config identity does not matter after setup. Apply guardrail worsened but remains near parity.
asi.next_action_hint: Confirm this split-copy choice before keeping more changes, then test the opposite split only if needed.

description: No-code confirmation of noref opts-only deepcopy split

asi.hypothesis: Confirm opts-only noref deepcopy improvement before stacking more changes.
asi.rollback_reason: No code change was made and delta_us reran near baseline (-77.56us vs previous -90.75us), so the opts-only split improvement is likely noisy.
asi.next_action_hint: Treat noref split choices as noise-prone. Do not stack further deepcopy micro changes without stronger repeated evidence.

description: Revert opts noref deepcopy to regular deepcopy after noisy confirmation

asi.hypothesis: If opts-only noref was noise, regular deepcopy should be as good or better under the stable paired startup benchmark and safer for preserving reference identity.
asi.rollback_reason: Regular deepcopy also measured near baseline and did not improve primary delta_us versus the noref opts-only commit's best run, so this revert was not kept.
asi.next_action_hint: Current code still has opts-only noref deepcopy. Consider reverting it later only for maintainability if performance target is already satisfied.

description: Use noref deepcopy only for base config while keeping user opts deepcopy regular

asi.hypothesis: Opposite split of the noref deepcopy experiment may show whether base config copy, not user opts copy, is the actual beneficiary under stable startup measurement.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary delta_us was slightly better than baseline but not better than current opts-only noref best, and command_us guardrail regressed badly to 84.09us. Not worth keeping.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not pursue more noref split experiments; measurements are noisy and guardrails vary.

description: Move status table construction into a lazily required status module

asi.hypothesis: Status reporting is not needed for setup; moving its larger table-building body out of core.lua should reduce core parse/load time without changing public status behavior.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary delta_us did not improve enough versus current best and added private helper surface plus a new module; extra indirection/risk is not worth the small noisy signal.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not split status alone. A broader public API split would need larger confirmed gains and careful tests.

description: Replace core-local setup time validation pattern matching with byte parsing

asi.hypothesis: Manual byte parsing can validate HH:MM times without string.match or tonumber during setup, improving the 24-entry schedule scenario while preserving accepted formats.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary delta_us did not improve versus current best enough to justify much more complex validation code; maintainability is worse.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep string.match/tonumber validate_time. Do not retry manual byte parsing.

description: Probe upper-bound startup savings from skipping focus autocmd registration entirely

asi.hypothesis: Focus autocmd registration may be a sizable setup cost; removing it entirely estimates the maximum possible win before attempting a correct conditional design.
asi.rollback_reason: Correctness checks failed because focus autocmd behavior is covered by tests and required for system-background polling. This probe is not keepable as-is.
asi.benchmark_signal: delta_us improved to -108.05us, suggesting autocmd registration is a real cost, but removing it would be incorrect and risks overfitting to non-system benchmark configs.
asi.next_action_hint: Only pursue conditional focus autocmd registration if adding tests for system backgrounds, persisted state, reload, and enable/disable. Otherwise leave unconditional autocmds.

description: Conditionally register focus autocmds only when system backgrounds are possible

asi.hypothesis: Focus autocmds are only needed when a config can request background='system'; non-system configs can skip registration while tests cover the system-background path.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary delta_us regressed badly from current best to -60.84us. Schedule scanning and sync logic outweighed avoiding autocmd API calls, even though correctness checks passed with updated tests.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not pursue conditional focus autocmd registration in this code shape. Keep unconditional focus autocmds despite probe showing an upper-bound speedup.

name: Apply speed with schedule runtime split exploration

description: Baseline apply speed before schedule runtime split exploration

asi.hypothesis: Switch back to apply_delta_us to explore schedule runtime split while startup and command remain guardrails.
asi.benchmark_change: Metric changed to apply_delta_us; same broadened four-scenario benchmark, paired apply delta, stable startup iterations retained.
asi.next_action_hint: Try splitting schedule runtime code from public validation wrapper only if public require('colorful-times.schedule') API can remain unchanged.

name: Apply speed with alternating paired measurement order

description: Baseline after alternating paired startup/apply measurement order

asi.hypothesis: Alternating Colorful Times/minimal order inside paired startup and apply samples should reduce ordering bias without favoring either implementation.
asi.benchmark_change: Metric implementation changed; apply_delta_us and delta_us now alternate CT/minimal order per paired sample.
asi.next_action_hint: With apply nearly at parity under alternating pairs, only keep schedule runtime split if it gives a clearly repeated improvement without hurting startup.

description: No-code confirmation of alternating paired apply baseline

asi.hypothesis: Confirm the alternating-order paired apply baseline before investing in schedule runtime split.
asi.rollback_reason: No production change was made and apply_delta_us reran worse than baseline (3.51us vs 0.35us), confirming remaining apply differences are small and noisy.
asi.next_action_hint: Avoid large schedule-runtime refactors unless they provide clear code simplicity or repeated >5us apply improvement.

description: Move open() wrapper from core.lua to init.lua to reduce core parse size

asi.hypothesis: TUI open wrapper is not needed by setup/apply; moving it out of core.lua could shrink core parse/load slightly while preserving command behavior.
asi.rollback_reason: Correctness checks failed, likely because lazy-loading expectations/tests depend on open being a core-provided lazy key. The measured apply_delta_us also worsened versus baseline.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep open defined in core.lua. Any broader public API split needs explicit test updates and larger expected gains.

description: Probe core-only schedule runtime split avoiding public schedule module load

asi.hypothesis: Core hot paths may only need a smaller runtime schedule helper, avoiding load/parse of public schedule validation functions during first apply while public color-times.schedule remains unchanged.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary improvement was only 0.20us versus baseline, far below observed no-code noise, while adding duplicated schedule logic and worsening command_us from 55.38us to 61.66us. Not a maintainable keepable win.
asi.next_action_hint: Prune schedule-runtime split unless redesigned to remove duplication and deliver repeated >5us apply improvement.

description: Localize default config table in resolve_theme_parts hot path

asi.hypothesis: Binding cfg.default to a local should reduce repeated table lookups in apply/resolve without changing behavior.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary apply_delta_us regressed from 0.35us to 2.40us; extra local did not help under the benchmark.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep current direct cfg.default lookups. Avoid table-lookup micro-optimizations in resolve_theme_parts unless paired with structural simplification.

name: Startup structural split after apply parity

description: Baseline startup structural split segment after apply parity

asi.hypothesis: Apply parity is noise-limited; switch primary back to stable paired startup delta for remaining structural split ideas while preserving apply and command guardrails.
asi.benchmark_change: Metric changed from apply_delta_us to delta_us; workload remains the four-scenario alternating paired benchmark with five startup cycles per sample.
asi.next_action_hint: Only test coherent startup structural splits; avoid one-method open/status moves already tried.

description: Group default option validation around local table

asi.hypothesis: Grouping opts.default validation under one local should avoid repeated nested table lookups during setup without changing validation behavior.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary delta_us regressed from -107.79us to -77.90us; this rewrite was slower despite being readable.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep current repeated opts.default checks. Avoid validation-locality rewrites unless they remove work, not just lookups.

description: Lazy access vim.uv only in timer start paths

asi.hypothesis: Removing the top-level uv local may shrink core setup load; timers can access vim.uv only when started later.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary delta_us regressed from -107.79us to -93.67us. The top-level uv local is not a startup bottleneck and local access is clearer for timer paths.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep local uv = vim.uv. Avoid single top-level lookup removals.

description: Use numeric loop for setup schedule validation

asi.hypothesis: Setup validation walks a Lua array; a numeric loop can reduce iterator overhead for scheduled configs, especially the 24-entry scenario, without changing validation behavior or public API.
asi.result: delta_us improved from -107.79us to -112.00us. Apply guardrail also improved to -1.27us and command_us improved versus segment baseline.
asi.tradeoff: Change is a small style shift from ipairs to numeric indexing in setup validation only. Need no-code confirmation because improvement is near the noise threshold.
asi.next_action_hint: Run a no-code confirmation before stacking more setup validation loop changes.

description: No-code confirmation of numeric setup schedule validation loop

asi.hypothesis: Confirm the numeric setup schedule validation loop before stacking more validation-loop changes because first improvement was near noise threshold.
asi.rollback_reason: No production change was made and delta_us reran worse (-86.81us), so the -112.00us result is not confirmed. The numeric loop remains kept only because it is simple and was already committed; treat its speed signal as weak.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not stack more numeric-loop rewrites based only on the first run. Consider reverting later for style if more confirmations stay worse.

description: Revert numeric validation loop back to ipairs

asi.hypothesis: Because numeric-loop confirmation was weak, reverting to idiomatic ipairs may be neutral or better under another sample.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary delta_us worsened to -81.43us, so the revert was not kept. Current code keeps the numeric validation loop despite weak confirmation.
asi.next_action_hint: Leave the numeric loop for now but do not infer that other ipairs-to-numeric rewrites are beneficial.

description: Bind opts.schedule local before validation loop

asi.hypothesis: A local opts_schedule could avoid repeated nested table lookups in setup schedule validation after the numeric-loop change.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary delta_us regressed to -83.34us. Extra local/rewrite did not help and the numeric loop's weak signal should not be extended.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep the committed numeric loop exactly as-is; avoid further schedule validation loop micro-edits.

name: Stable startup delta with 31 paired samples

description: Baseline startup delta with 31 paired samples

asi.hypothesis: Increase sample count from 21 to 31 to reduce benchmark noise before more sub-10us startup experiments.
asi.benchmark_change: CT_BENCH_SAMPLES default changed from 21 to 31; workload and primary delta_us remain otherwise unchanged.
asi.next_action_hint: Use this as new noise-reduced baseline; require repeated improvements for small code changes.

description: Localize core autocmd API functions

asi.hypothesis: Binding nvim_create_augroup, nvim_clear_autocmds, and nvim_create_autocmd to locals could reduce setup-time API lookup overhead.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary delta_us regressed slightly from -92.01us to -90.43us and command/apply guardrails also worsened. Top-level local bindings did not pay for themselves.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep direct vim.api autocmd calls in core; avoid further API-localization micro-opts.

description: Hoist focus autocmd callbacks and option tables

asi.hypothesis: Reusing module-local focus callbacks and autocmd option tables should avoid per-setup closure/table allocation while preserving unconditional registration behavior.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary delta_us regressed from -92.01us to -84.47us and apply/command guardrails worsened. Extra top-level functions/tables outweighed setup allocation savings.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep inline focus autocmd callbacks/options in setup. Autocmd allocation hoisting is not worthwhile.

description: Hoist deferred setup callback into module-local function

asi.hypothesis: Setup always schedules the same deferred startup work; passing a module-local function to vim.defer_fn avoids allocating a fresh closure per setup without deferring additional required work.
asi.result: delta_us improved from -92.01us to -104.99us under 31 samples. Apply guardrail was stable/slightly better; command_us worsened to 61.55us and needs monitoring.
asi.tradeoff: Moves deferred setup body out of M.setup, but behavior stays synchronous-equivalent except for reusing the function object.
asi.next_action_hint: Run no-code confirmation; keep only if delta improvement repeats and command regression stays acceptable.

description: No-code confirmation of hoisted deferred setup callback

asi.hypothesis: Confirm the module-local deferred setup callback improvement under 31-sample benchmark before stacking more setup closure changes.
asi.rollback_reason: No production change was made and delta_us reran much worse (-69.34us); first improvement is not confirmed. Apply guardrail also worsened to 6.41us.
asi.next_action_hint: Consider reverting hoisted deferred callback if another confirmation/revert probe shows it is noise or hurts apply.

description: Revert hoisted deferred setup callback back inline

asi.hypothesis: Because no-code confirmation was poor, reverting the hoisted deferred setup callback may be neutral or better.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary delta_us worsened to -77.10us and apply_delta_us worsened to 7.18us, so the revert was not kept. Current code keeps the hoisted deferred callback despite weak confirmation.
asi.next_action_hint: Leave hoisted callback for now, but treat its speed signal as noisy; do not stack more closure-hoisting changes.

description: Explicit schema-field merge for default config

asi.hypothesis: Replacing generic vim.tbl_deep_extend for default config with explicit documented-field assignment could reduce setup merge overhead while preserving public config schema.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary delta_us regressed badly to -63.51us and apply_delta_us worsened to 7.42us. Generic deep_extend is faster/safer in this context or noise strongly disfavors the rewrite.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep vim.tbl_deep_extend for default config. Do not pursue manual config merge without a broader redesign and tests for unknown/nil behavior.

description: Localize vim.deepcopy and vim.tbl_deep_extend in setup

asi.hypothesis: Binding vim.deepcopy and vim.tbl_deep_extend to locals may reduce repeated global lookups in setup copy/merge.
asi.rollback_reason: Delta improvement versus segment baseline was small/noisy, worse than the prior hoisted-callback run, while command_us worsened sharply to 64.67us and apply_delta_us worsened to 3.89us.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep direct vim.deepcopy/vim.tbl_deep_extend calls. Avoid setup helper localization unless it delivers a repeated larger delta win without guardrail regressions.

description: Inline background validation comparisons

asi.hypothesis: Inline comparisons for light/dark/system might avoid table lookup overhead in setup validation for default and schedule backgrounds.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary delta_us regressed badly to -55.84us and apply guardrail worsened. The lookup table is faster/clearer here.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep VALID_BACKGROUNDS table lookups. Do not inline background validation.

description: No-code stability check after hoisted deferred callback

asi.hypothesis: Estimate current 31-sample noise after several weak/failed setup micro-optimizations.
asi.rollback_reason: No production change was made and delta_us remained worse than the original 31-sample baseline (-82.74us vs -92.01us), adding evidence that hoisted deferred callback may be noisy despite failed revert probe.
asi.next_action_hint: Run a focused revert of both weak kept changes (numeric validation loop and hoisted defer callback) together; if baseline code wins, keep the revert for maintainability.

description: Revert both weak kept setup changes together

asi.hypothesis: Reverting both weak kept changes (numeric validation loop and hoisted deferred callback) may restore the cleaner 31-sample baseline if their apparent wins were noise.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary delta_us worsened to -87.73us compared with the 31-sample baseline -92.01us and the combined revert did not justify removing committed changes.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep both weak changes for now because revert probes are worse, but note confirmation remains noisy. Move to larger structural ideas or stop micro-opts.

name: Stable startup delta with 31 samples and 9 startup iterations

description: Baseline startup delta with 31 samples and 9 startup iterations

asi.hypothesis: Increase startup iterations per sample from 5 to 9 to reduce startup noise for current startup-focused segment.
asi.benchmark_change: CT_BENCH_STARTUP_ITERS default changed from 5 to 9; samples remain 31 and workload unchanged.
asi.next_action_hint: Use this steadier startup baseline for final weak-change decisions; compare candidate changes against it rather than earlier noisier segments.

description: Revert weak setup loop and deferred callback changes under 31x9 benchmark

asi.hypothesis: With the steadier 31x9 startup benchmark, the two weak kept changes may show as noise; reverting both restores simpler code and may improve startup delta.
asi.result: delta_us improved strongly from -62.12us to -88.67us. apply_delta_us also improved to -1.22us. command_us worsened to 63.02us but remains near prior noise/guardrail levels.
asi.tradeoff: Reverts numeric validation loop back to idiomatic ipairs and inlines the deferred setup callback again, improving maintainability.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep this revert unless a no-code confirmation is catastrophically worse; do not reintroduce either weak change.

description: No-code confirmation after reverting weak setup changes

asi.hypothesis: Confirm the reverted weak setup changes under the steadier 31x9 benchmark.
asi.result: No-code confirmation improved delta_us further to -95.20us. Apply stayed favorable at -1.84us and command_us returned to 56.76us, removing the prior command guardrail concern.
asi.next_action_hint: Treat the revert as confirmed. Continue only with larger structural ideas; avoid revisiting numeric validation loops or hoisted deferred callbacks.

description: Split rare enable/disable/reload actions out of core

asi.hypothesis: Moving rare command actions into actions.lua might shrink core parse/setup path while preserving behavior through private hooks.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary delta_us fell back to -63.78us versus the confirmed current -95.20us, apply_delta_us worsened to 7.01us, and the private hook module added significant complexity. Not keepable.
asi.next_action_hint: Prune broader action split idea unless a much cleaner API boundary is designed; wrappers/private hook tables are too costly and complex.

description: Replace timers table with separate schedule and poll timer locals

asi.hypothesis: Separate _schedule_timer and _poll_timer locals may avoid a module-load table allocation and runtime _timers table lookups without changing timer behavior.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary delta_us was worse than the current confirmed HEAD signal (-86.06us vs -95.20us) and apply/command guardrails worsened versus confirmation. Code was not worth keeping despite being structurally simple.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep the _timers table. Avoid module-load allocation micro-opts unless they repeatedly beat the confirmed 31x9 result.

description: No-code stability check after pruning exhausted ideas

asi.hypothesis: With obvious ideas exhausted, rerun the current HEAD to estimate 31x9 noise and avoid chasing stale micro-optimizations.
asi.rollback_reason: No production change was made and delta_us reran worse than the prior confirmed HEAD result (-67.41us vs -95.20us), showing substantial residual noise. Autoresearch docs remain preserved.
asi.next_action_hint: Only keep future changes with repeated large improvements; consider benchmark/reporting improvements rather than code micro-opts.

description: Add paired-delta spread metrics to benchmark output

asi.hypothesis: Report startup/apply paired-delta p25/p75/IQR to quantify noise before keeping more sub-10us changes.
asi.rollback_reason: Benchmark harness crashed because collect_apply_pair still returned only three values, leaving apply_delta_p75_us nil during IQR calculation.
asi.next_action_hint: Reapply diagnostics carefully by updating both collect_startup_pair and collect_apply_pair return paths.

description: Report paired startup/apply delta spread diagnostics

asi.hypothesis: Adding p25/p75/IQR for paired startup and apply deltas will quantify residual benchmark noise and reduce overfitting to sub-10us single-run changes.
asi.result: Benchmark now reports delta_iqr_us=37.45 and apply_delta_iqr_us=19.54, confirming that tiny micro-optimizations are below noise. Primary delta_us remains faster than minimal at -85.98us.
asi.benchmark_change: No production code changed; benchmark output gained startup/apply paired-delta spread metrics. A previous crash attempt was fixed by updating both paired collectors.
asi.next_action_hint: Use IQR as a guardrail: avoid keeping future code changes unless improvement is repeated and large relative to spread, or the change improves maintainability.

description: No-code confirmation with paired-delta spread diagnostics

asi.hypothesis: Confirm current HEAD after adding spread diagnostics and capture noise envelope for future decisions.
asi.result: delta_us reran at -93.82us, close to the prior confirmed -95.20us. delta_iqr_us remained large at 47.91us, so future code changes need large/repeated wins.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not chase micro-optimizations. Only try changes that remove meaningful work from core load/setup or improve benchmark quality.

description: Report per-scenario startup delta diagnostics

asi.hypothesis: Adding per-scenario startup deltas will show whether remaining startup variance or regressions are concentrated in specific representative configs without changing the aggregate primary metric.
asi.result: Benchmark now reports scenario deltas. First run: empty=-94.11us, two_entry=-80.39us, disabled=-88.16us, hourly=-42.07us. Hourly schedule is the weakest parity scenario but still faster than the minimal reference.
asi.benchmark_change: Added diagnostic-only per-scenario startup delta metrics; primary delta_us calculation and workload are unchanged.
asi.next_action_hint: If any future structural work is considered, target broad core-load/setup cost rather than schedule micro-opts: hourly is weakest, but schedule-specific experiments already regressed and are noise-prone.
